allowlist._comment: Allowlist for a2web Rego policies (Rego engine is a2kit's; the `a2kit` namespace is the policy bundle's, not project-specific). Each entry needs a non-empty 'reason'. Naming conventions intentionally converge across the codebase — that's not duplication, it's the project's shape.

body_dup.reason: per-tier plugin manifest factory convention: zyte + firecrawl each key-gate their own tier (different settings attr + tier class). The identical shape IS the plugin pattern, not extractable duplication — lifting it would couple two independent optional tiers.

name_collision.reason: every Resource class follows the lazy-init pattern: sync __init__, internal _lock, idempotent _ensure(), close(). Convergent name is the codebase convention (cookie_jar, llm_resource, browser_pool, http_cache). Renaming would obscure the pattern.
factory-helper convention; convergent name signals 'internal factory used by the module's public builder'.
parser/argparse-CLI convention; each module owns its own private parser; convergence is the idiom.
handler-shape convention: each site handler has its own private loader pair. Lifting to a shared helper would lose handler-specific URL shape encoding.
handler-shape convention: each handler renders its own DOM into markdown. Different sites have different DOM, so renderers can't share.
handler-shape convention: each handler's tier-0 candidate extraction has site-specific selectors.
string-truncate helper; appears in live_sink (terminal column fitting) and llm_extract/extractor (token-budget fitting). Different domains, same name.
tier convention: archive.py and browser.py each convert their own HTML payload into markdown.
tier convention: jina.py and raw.py each map HTTP status to FetchVerdict. Statuses overlap but tier semantics differ.
host-matching convention: cookie_jar matches request host against cookie-jar entries; proxy_routing matches request host against proxy-pool entries.
CLI entrypoint convention: every llm_eval CLI module exposes `_amain(args)` wrapped by anyio.run in `main`.

github_actions_vendor.reason: official Docker org actions (login/metadata/build-push/setup-buildx) for the GHCR release workflow

github_actions_vendor_unpinned.reason: first-party GitHub actions; mutation risk accepted for solo repo
uv vendor; setup-uv tag is acceptable for solo-repo CI
official Docker org actions; major-version tag acceptable for solo-repo CI (same posture as actions/astral-sh)
